summary: This field passes. There is a real cluster around body or identity instability and institutional processing of altered people, often in near-future or blue-economy settings, but the slate still spans clearly different novel shapes. It includes intimate metaphysical romance, family road hustle, erotic labor thriller, forensic tragedy, infrastructure saga, pilgrimage epic, and feral social satire.
strengths: Multiple distinct engines appear: route odyssey, claims-desk noir, forensic procedure, long-span love story, infrastructure saga, convoy romance, and grotesque satire.
The social geometries are meaningfully different, from family-in-a-cab to worker-versus-corporation to lovers on a route to a caretaker embedded in a failing community.
The consequence structures vary: some premises intensify intimate selfhood crises while others widen into scandal, scarcity, migration, liability, or civic dependency.
The field is genre-fair rather than prestige-narrow, with comedy, erotic charge, grotesquerie, procedure, melodrama, and speculative weirdness all represented.
similarity_risks: Several candidates hinge on duplicated, transferred, or unstable personhood in altered bodies.
A noticeable share of the field lives in coastal or floating near-future ecologies with labor or corporate pressure.
Institutional worker protagonists processing uncanny evidence recur enough to form a visible mini-cluster.

overlap_pairs.reason: Both are procedural biotech novels built on workers adjudicating whether a person in a manufactured body is really the same self.
Both center on death administration on the water, where official records fail because bodies refuse to stay stable.
Both are coastal eco-erotic premises about a woman monetizing forbidden access to a compromised landscape while class power turns predatory.
Both use low-level labor inside a commercialized body-duplication system to ask who gets replaced, repaired, or quietly exploited.
Both are checkpoint-driven escort journeys where each stop re-tests identity, obligation, and who is entitled to passage or relief.
